title: The One With Ross's Wedding - The Uncut Version

lines.speaker: Part I Written by
lines.text: Michael Borkow

lines.speaker: Part II Teleplay by
lines.text: Shana Goldberg-Meehan & Scott Silveri

lines.speaker: Part II Story by
lines.text: Jill Condon & Amy Toomin

lines.speaker: Part III Written by
lines.text: Seth Kurland

lines.speaker: Part I Transcribed by
lines.text: Eric Aasen & Marita Bakken

lines.speaker: Part II Transcribed by
lines.text: Aaron D. Miller & Marita Bakken

lines.speaker: Part III Transcribed by
lines.text: Eric Aasen & Marita Bakken

{Transcriber's Note: This is the Uncut Version of Ross's second wedding it includes episodes 423, 424, and 501.  It also includes some previously cut parts not shown in the U.S.  Those parts are shown in blue text.}
Chandler and Joey's, Joey and Chandler are getting ready for the flight to London and Monica comes running in.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Guys, hurry up! The flight leaves in four hours! It could take time to get a taxi! There could be traffic! The plane could leave early! When we get to London, there could be a line at customs! Come on!! (She runs back to her apartment.)

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Six-hour trip to London. That's a lot of Monica.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: What do you got there?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Condoms, dude!
Joey (looks at the box): How come the writing is in Spanish?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Don't read my condoms!
[Cut to the girls' apartment, Monica is putting things into her purse as Phoebe and Rachel watch.]

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Passport, check! (As she puts away each item, she says check.) Camera, check! Traveller's cheques, check!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Who are you saying "check" too?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Myself. Y'know for remembering to pack a thing. Yeah, you do a good thing, you get a check! (pause) My mom does it, I never realised it was weird.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Yeah, my mom used to put her head in the oven. Well, actually, she only did it the one time. But it was pretty weird.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: (entering) Hey!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Hey!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Hey! Are you ready yet?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Yep! You got the tickets?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Oh! Got 'em right here, (Pats his coat pocket) check!
[Cut to the guys' apartment.]

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: It's all London, baby! Here we go. (He takes a picture of a less than enthused Chandler and starts towards the girls' apartment.)

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: You got your passport?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Yeah, in my third drawer on my dresser. You don't want to lose that.
Chandler glares at him. At first Joey doesn't know why, it takes him a little bit to figure it out.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Ohh!! (Runs to his room.)

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: There it is.
Opening Credits
Monica and Rachel's, continued from earlier, Monica is telling Phoebe where everything is.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Okay, if you need the vacuum, it's in my closet on the left-hand side. Ah, the garbage bags are next to the refrigerator...

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Okay, okay, but Rachel's gonna be here too, can't I just ask her this stuff?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Yeah, okay, give that a try!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: (entering, with Joey) All right! Let's do it!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Woo-hoo!!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Yeah, cheerie-o!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: London baby!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Okay, 'cause that's not gonna get annoying.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: (louder) London baby!!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Hey, y'know what? I was wrong.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Well, we're all here! I guess we should get going!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Ohhh, I wanna come over there and give you a hug and wish you luck on your wedding, but I don't-can't get up.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Oh, I'll-I'll come hug you.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Great! Yeah, could you bring me the newspaper?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Yeah. (He does so and Phoebe hugs him.)

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Oh, have a great wedding!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Thanks.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Oh, hey, Chandler I wanna hug you too!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Hey! (Trots over)

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Oh, and great! You might as well bring me my book, it's on the counter in your apartment.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Oh. (Goes and gets Phoebe's book as Rachel comes in from her room.)

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: (to Rachel) So, we're off.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Have fun!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Thanks! (They hug.) Ugh, I can't believe you're not gonna be there!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh, I know.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: So-so come! Why don't you come?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: What?!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: To London! Come to London. Please? It'll mean so much to me.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Yeah, well, I gotta work, I'm sorry.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Why-why can't you take a couple of days off?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Because, I can't! Ross, I told you, no. I can't.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: This is my wedding.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: All right, y'know what? We really are late! Let's go! Let's go! Let's go!!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Fine. You'll-you'll watch it on video when we get back.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: (entering, with Phoebe's book.) Here you go Phoebe! Here you go Phobo! Phewbedo! Phaybobo.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: (laughs) Thank you.
Chandler kneels down with his arms spread waiting for his hug.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Thank you. (She pats him on his head.)

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: All right, let's go! Bye, Pheebs!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Bye, Pheebs!
They all start out, Rachel gives each one a kiss, and says "bye." In the hall, Joey says...

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: London baby!! (And Rachel slowly closes the door, sadly.)

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Oh, do you need a hug? You don't have to bring me anything!

And with that, television history is made as, for the first time ever, an entire show moves it's entire production to an entirely different country to make a single episode. We get shots of Buckingham Palace, London Bridge, Big Ben, and the London Marriott as Joey and Chandler exit.
Street in front of the London Marriott, Joey and Chandler exit. Joey is carrying a video camera and is shooting Chandler.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Come on! Do something!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: I am, I'm ignoring you.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Okay, here! (Gives him the camera.) I wanna be the on camera guy. All right, first stop, Westminster Abbey. (Joey folds out his "pop-up" map of London. All of the major landmarks pop-up like in a pop-up book.)

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Oh, what the hell is that?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: It's London, baby! All right, the hotel's here. (Points to the map.) Wait. No, we wanna go... No. I know. (Sets the map down.) I'm gonna have to go into the map. (So Joey literally steps into the map.)

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Okay, if you see a little version of me in there? Kill it!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: I got it! (Picks up the map and starts walking.) Here we go.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Okay. Listen-listen, we're not gonna have to walk this way the entire time are we?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Shhh! (Pause) Man, you made me lose it! (He goes into the map again.)
Another street, somewhere along the River Thames, Ross, Emily, and Monica are walking to where they're gonna get married. Emily is relating the troubles with the caterer.

lines.speaker: Emily
lines.text: ...and that was all before 10 o'clock. The caterer rang and said it was going to be Chicken Kiev instead of Chicken Tarragon. And then the florist phoned to say there aren't any tulips. Oh, and the chilliest has carpel-tunel syndrome. We're not gonna be...

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Whoa-whoa-whoa! Emily, (Gives her the time-out signal.) honey, okay?

lines.speaker: Emily
lines.text: Well, up yours too!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: What?!

lines.speaker: Emily
lines.text: Oh, that's not what it means?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: No! No! That's-that's time-out!

lines.speaker: Emily
lines.text: Sorry.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Sweetie, you gotta relax. Everything's gonna be great, okay? Come on. Come on.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Chicken Kiev?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Um-hmm! Doesn't that sound delicious at the last minute?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Yeah, y'know, but something like salmon which would be so much more elegant than the chicken. And, you wouldn't have to worry about the salmonella. (Ross pushes her.) But of course with salmon you'd have to worry about the chickenella... So, I can't wait to see this place you're getting married!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Yeah. Yeah. Yes, this place is beautiful. Emily's parents got married here.

lines.speaker: Emily
lines.text: I still can't believe they're tearing it down. It really is the most lovely building you'll ever see. I mean it's over... (She stops suddenly, when she sees that demolition has already started.) Oh my God!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: It's nice.

lines.speaker: Emily
lines.text: Oh. Oh. (She starts running towards the building.)
[Cut to inside.]

lines.speaker: Emily
lines.text: (running in, with Ross) Oh. Oh my God. How can this be happening? What are we going to do?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: It's all right! Everything's gonna be all right.

lines.speaker: Emily
lines.text: How's it gonna be all right?!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Uh-huh, I see that.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: (entering) Okay, I talked to the guy with the shovel and I found out what happened.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: What? What?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: They tore it down a few days early.

Chandler and Joey are sitting on the top floor on one of the red coaches in London that doesn't have a roof. Joey is very enthusiastic and is pointing at all the different sights. Chandler is really embarrassed and tries to make him stop. Afterwards, Joey tries to film Chandler with his video camera, and Chandler obviously hates this. Then, Joey falls asleep, and Chandler tries to take the video camera away from him. However, Joey is holding onto it (in his sleep) and won't let it go. Chandler pulls until Joey lets it go, causing Chandler to fall out of his seat.
Westminster Abbey, Joey and Chandler have successfully navigated the streets of London and are approaching the Abbey.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: All right! Westminster Abbey! Hands down, best Abbey I've ever seen. Hey! (Pushes Chandler in front of the camera.) Okay. What do you think of the Abbey, Chandler?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: I think it's great. It's great. Y'know, they're thinking of changing the name of this place.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Really? To what?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: To Put the Camera Away!!!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Man, you are Westminster Crabby. (He starts chasing Chandler towards the Abbey.)
A bridal shop somewhere in London. Monica is trying on her red bridesmaid dress, and Emily is watching. A dressmaker is working on Monica's dress.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Don't worry. You'll find some place to get married.
The dressmaker puts a pin in the dress, but accidentally pricks Monica with it.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Oowww!!

lines.speaker: Dressmaker
lines.text: Sorry. Lucky this dress is red!

lines.speaker: Emily
lines.text: We can't find another place. Then we can't have the wedding! I've ordered chicken and salad and-and cake for 150. What should be the most wonderful day of my life is rapidly turning into just Sunday with a spectacular amount of food!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: You're gonna find a place. But even if - God forbid - you didn't.. So you postpone the wedding. Is that really so bad? I mean, think about it. You could take all the time you needed to get everything that you want. You could have tulips, you could have salmon.... Mmmmm... Salmon!!

lines.speaker: Emily
lines.text: I suppose it wouldn't be the end of the world... Oh, thank you!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: But either way... You're gonna get married, you're gonna be happy, my mom's gonna like you better than me.
Emily smiles a bit and looks uncomfortable.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Oh, god... She's told you that already, didn't she?!
Monica and Rachel's, Phoebe is trying to move off of the couch as Rachel enters.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Oh. Oh.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh, honey! Don't get up! What do you need?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Oh, no. Oh, nothing.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Come on! I am here to take care of you! What do you need? Anything.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Okay, I have a wedgie.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Okay, that is all you.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: So-so, what do you want for lunch?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh, I don't know. I guess we have to eat.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Yeah, I do. What's the matter?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: I'm just bummed about the way I left things with Ross. I shouldn't have lied to him about having to work. He seemed so mad at me.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Eh, don't be so hard on yourself. If someone I was still in love with was getting married...

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: (interrupting) Still in love with?!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Yeah!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: I'm not in love with Ross!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Oh. No. No. Good! Yeah, me neither.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Phoebe, I'm going to Ross's wedding because he is my ex-boyfriend and that would be really uncomfortable. Not because, I'm still in love with him! I mean, hey, y'know, I like Ross as much as the next guy, y'know? Clearly I have feelings for him, but feelings don't mean love! I mean, I still have loving feelings for Ross. Yeah! But, I have, I have continuing feelings of love, but that doesn't mean that-that I'm still in love with him. Y'know? I-I have sexual feelings for him, but I do love him-Ohh! Oh my God! Oh my-why didn't you tell me?!!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: We thought you knew!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: We?!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Yeah, we all know! We talk about it all the time!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: You all know? Does Ross know?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Oh no, Ross doesn't know anything.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh, I can not believe you didn't tell me!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Well, because we thought you knew!! It's so obvious! God, that would be like telling Monica, "Hey, you like things clean." Or, y'know, "Hey, Joey, you're gay."
Rachel stares at her.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: What?!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Oh, please! She's always got a broom in her hand!
A park in London, Joey and Chandler walk up to a souvenir stand.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Hey!

lines.speaker: The Vendor
lines.text: So, what are you guys in the market for? We've got uh, scarves, tulip post cards...

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Check this out? Huh? (Joey has this big tall hat with a British flag on the front of it.) Yeah. That's the stuff. What do you think?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Well, I don't have to buy that, "I'm with stupid" T-shirt anymore.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Well, I like it. Here you go. (He pays for the hat.)

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: All right, look, you're not really gonna buy that are you? Don't you think you've embarrassed me enough for one day?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Oh, I embarrass you?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: How can I answer that when I'm pretending I don't know you?

lines.speaker: The Vendor
lines.text: He's just jealous. You'll fit right in; all Londoners wear them!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Oh really? Then how come no one here is wearing them?

lines.speaker: The Vendor
lines.text: (looks around) They're all tourists.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: All right, look, if you insist on wearing that, in public, y'know, you're gonna spend the rest of the afternoon all by yourself.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Oh yeah? (Puts the hat on.) If you're gonna make me choose between you and the hat? I choose the hat.

lines.speaker: The Vendor
lines.text: Good choice.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Thanks.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Okay, wait. All right, that's it, okay, I'm out of here. I am not going to be embarrassed anymore! (He trips over a box, falls into a flower stand and walks away trying to be cool.)
A man walks up to Joey and stares at Chandler and the flower stand. Joey turns around to introduce himself.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: How you doin'? Joey Tribbiani, America.
Joey shakes his hand. The man looks at him and his hat.
The hotel, Ross's room, Emily is entering.

lines.speaker: Emily
lines.text: Hello?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Hey! I just spoke to your dad, and you know what? He seems to think we'll be able to find a new place for the wedding.

lines.speaker: Emily
lines.text: We don't have to.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Whoa-whoa-what? You found a place?

lines.speaker: Emily
lines.text: No. But, Monica and I were talking, and-and I was so upset about the hall being knocked down, and she suggested that we put the wedding off for a bit.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: She said what?

lines.speaker: Emily
lines.text: She said, "If I'm not gonna be happy getting married somewhere that we find in a day, well then we should just postpone it."

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Postpone it? Emily, do you think Monica realises how much our parents spent on this wedding? Do you my sister's teeny-tiny little brain comprehends that people took time out of their lives to fly thousands of miles to be here, huh? (He puts his pants on backwards.) This isn't right.

lines.speaker: Emily
lines.text: I realise that people are going to be disappointed. But, I'm sure they'll come back when we can do it right.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: I can't ask people to do that? Would you ask people to do that? (Holds out his pants)

lines.speaker: Emily
lines.text: Don't you point your pants at me! (She throws them on the floor.) We have no choice! Anywhere that's half-decent would've be booked months ago, Ross don't you understand? This is our wedding I'm talking about.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: The only thing I understand is; postponing it is not an option. This is when we're getting married.

lines.speaker: Emily
lines.text: So what are you saying? It's now or never?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: No. I'm saying it's now. (He starts putting on his pants, backwards again.)

lines.speaker: Emily
lines.text: Or?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: There's no 'or' in mind. What is wrong with these pants?!!

lines.speaker: Emily
lines.text: It's not the pants. It's you that is backwards. And if, and if you don't understand how important this is to me, well then, perhaps we shouldn't get married at all! (She storms out.)

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: (chasing her, trying to zip up his pants. His got them on right now.) No, wait! Emily! No, wait, stop! Emily, please-(He catches something important in the zipper and howls like a little boy and falls to the floor.)
Commercial Break
Monica and Rachel's, Phoebe is on the couch as Rachel returns carrying a bunch of shopping bags.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Hey!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: (depressed) Hi.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: So, did shopping make you feel any better about Ross?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Manhattan does not have enough stores.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Well, I think I can help you get over him.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: You can?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Yeah. I just need you to bring me some photos of Ross.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Um-hmm.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: And a small piece of chocolate.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Okay.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: And a glass of tepid water. (She gathers up all of these things.)

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Ooh, is this one of those things where you throw it in a bag with some graveyard dirt and hang it from a north-facing tree?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Uh, only if you have the hiccups too. Yeah, the pictures are for you, the water and the chocolate is for me. I just didn't feel like getting up. Okay, I'm gonna show you a picture of Ross. Okay? And you're going to remember all of the bad things about him. All right? Really focus on his flaws.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: I can do that. I certainly did it when we were going out.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Okay. Umm, before we get started, I just wanna say for the record that I love Ross, I think he's such a great guy. Here. (Hands her the picture, Rachel grabs it out of her hand.) Okay, now, close your eyes. And imagine that you're with Ross okay and imagine that you're kissing him. And you're-you're running your hands all over his body. And then you run your hands through his hair, but eew-oh gross it's some kind of grease, it's-uck! Hah?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: I don't know, his uh, his hair never really bothered me that much, and y'know it was always more crunchy than it was greasy.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Okay, this is going to be harder than I thought. Umm, let's try some uh, aversion therapy.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Okay.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Okay?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: All right.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: So uh, now look at the picture...

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Okay.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: All right, and umm... (She grabs the picture and smacks her in the head.)

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Ow!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Okay, how do you feel now?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Well, I like you less!
Chandler and Joey's hotel room. Chandler is trying to kick his shoe into a trash can that is standing on the TV. He can't do it.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Well, this is just as boring in England.
Joey returns, still wearing his hat.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Hey.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Hey. (He nods at the hat.)

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Oh. (Takes off the hat.) Sorry!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: No-no-no, y'know what? I really shouldn't have said that you were embarrassing me, I mean that really wasn't cool. And if it makes you feel any better, I've had a really lousy day.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Me too.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Yeah?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Noo!! I've had the best day ever! Dude, check this out! Now, I'm gonna fastforward past the part with you, 'cause it is boring.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: (entering) Hey!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Sh-shh-shh! (He motions for her to watch something he has taped.)
[Cut to the tape Joey made in front of some famous place in London with a rather famous English-type person.]

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: (on tape) Okay, so say hi to my friend and tell him that you like the hat.

lines.speaker: Fergie
lines.text: (Yep, Sarah, the Duchess of York) Okay, so umm, what's your friend's name?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: (on tape) Oh, Chandler.

lines.speaker: Fergie
lines.text: Hi Chandler! (Waves)

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: That's... That's was...

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Oh my God!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: That's Fergie baby!!

lines.speaker: Fergie
lines.text: Joey says you don't really like his hat, but I think it's kinda dashing.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: (on tape) So, I hear you're single now...

lines.speaker: Fergie
lines.text: Yeah, ummm, I don't like the hat that much. (She smiles.)

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: How did you? How? How?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Well, I was trying to figure out how to get to Buckingham Palace, right? So, I'm in my map and-and...(Ross enters) Hey!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Hey!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Hi. I understand you had a little talk with Emily.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: (laughs) Yes, I did! And you are welcome!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Am I?! And was it your idea to postpone the wedding?!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Umm...

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: I'm gonna go to the bathroom.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Wait up! (They both run to the bathroom.)

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Hey-hey, since you're the 'fix-it' lady, here's a pickle, what do you do when the bride says she doesn't want to have the wedding at all?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: She said that?!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Yeah.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Why?!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: I don't know, I told her it was stupid to put off the wedding just because the hall was gone and she liked flipped out.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Oh my God. You're even dumber than I am!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Excuse me?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Ross, how long have you been planning this wedding?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: I don't know. A month?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Emily has probably been planning it since she was five! Ever since the first time she took a pillowcase and hung it off the back of her head. That's what we did! We dreamed about the perfect wedding, and the perfect place, with the perfect four-tiered wedding cake (Starting to cry), with the little people on top. (Ross gets thrown a box of Kleenex from the bathroom and he gives her one.) Thanks. But the most important part is that we had the perfect guy who understood just how important all that other stuff was.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: I had no idea. And that-that pillowcase thing, I thought you guys were just doing the flying nun.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Sometimes we were.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Come on. You gotta help me figure out what to do. Okay?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Okay.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Come on. (They leave and Chandler sticks his head out.)

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: That was pretty intense huh?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Yeah. (Pause) Hey, I hope Ross didn't think that we just went in there because we were uncomfortable being out here!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: (glares at him) I hope he did!
Monica and Rachel's. Phoebe is on the couch, and Rachel is sitting on the chair.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Ooh! I thought of a good flaw! Ross pops his gum!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh, right! (BEAT) Wait a minute, I do that too.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Yeah, I know. It drives me crazy.
The phone rings.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: I'll get it.
She answers it.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Hello.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: (on phone) Hey, Pheebs! It's Joey!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Hey, Joey! Hey! Ooh! Ooh! I just say someone on the-that looks just like you on the subway. And I was gonna go over and say 'hi!' but then I figured, he doesn't care if he looks like you.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: That just cost me four bucks. But uh listen, I just called to see how the chick and the duck are doing?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Ohh, they're having a great time with their Aunt Phoebe! Aunt Rachel hasn't been helpful at all. So, do you miss me?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Kinda, but I've just been having way too much fun.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: So you're not homesick yet?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: No, I don't think so.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: All right, the seven of us miss you.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Who's seven?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Y'know, me, Rachel, the birds, the babies...

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Ahh, the babies miss me?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Of course they do! Or I'm just really hungry.
There is a knock on Rachel and Phoebe's door.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Ooh, the pizza guy's here!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: What? You ordered pizza without me?!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Yeah. But y'know we were thinking about you, y'know we ordered the Joey Special.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Two pizzas?!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Yep! Okay, gotta go, talk to you later.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Wait, well, where did you get it from?!

Phoebe has already hung up, leaving Joey in the dark. So Joey decides to watch some TV and turns on a rerun of Cheers, with the theme song playing. At first, he's happy, but as the song progresses Joey gets depressed and homesick.
Ross and Emily's planned wedding place, Monica is dragging Emily in.

lines.speaker: Emily
lines.text: Monica, why have you brought me here of all places?!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: You'll see.

lines.speaker: Emily
lines.text: I tell you, this wedding is not going to happen.
At that Ross plugs in some Christmas lights to light the place up.

lines.speaker: Emily
lines.text: Oh God.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Okay? But-but imagine a lot more lights, okay? And-and y'know fewer bricks, and-and-and flowers, and candles...

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: And the musicians, look, they can go over here (Points to a little alcove), okay? And the chairs can face this way (Points), and... (Points to Ross) You go.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: But-but, if you don't love this, we'll do it in any other place at any other time. Really, it's fine, whatever you want.

lines.speaker: Emily
lines.text: It's perfect.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: And, I don't know, if it starts to rain...

lines.speaker: Emily
lines.text: Well then we'll get wet. (They kiss.)

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Ohh. And I don't even have a date.
Monica and Rachel's, Rachel is in her bedroom.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Pheebs?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Yeah?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Do you remember where the duck food is?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Yeah, it's in the guys' apartment under the sink. Why?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: (enters with a bag packed) Because I'm going to London.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: What?! What do you mean you're going to London?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Yeah, I have to tell Ross that I love him. Now honey, you take care, you don't have those babies until I get back. (Kisses her stomach.)

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: But what about all the "finding-his-flaws" stuff we've been doing?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Yeah, that didn't work.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: I-Rachel, you can't go! Ross loves Emily!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Yeah, I know, I know, I know he does. But I have to tell him how I feel! He deserves to have all the information and then he can make an informed decision.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: That's not why you're going! You're going because you hope he's gonna say, "Yeah, I love you too, Rach. Forget that British chippy."

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Ohh-Do you think he will?!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: No! Because he's in love with the British chippy! Look, Rachel, if you go, you're just gonna mess with his head and ruin his wedding! Y'know, it's too late! You missed you're chance! I'm sorry, I know this must be really hard, it's over.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Y'know what? No. It's not over until someone says, "I do." (Exits)

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: I do! I do! I do! (Chases her into the hall, but Rachel doesn't stop.) I do! (Gives up.) Ugh, like I can really chase you. I'm carrying a litter.
Commercial Break
Moving Shot towards The Waltham House. A phone is ringing.

lines.speaker: Housekeeper
lines.text: The Waltham Residence.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Oh...yes..is this..umm..Emily's Parents' house.

lines.speaker: Housekeeper
lines.text: This is the housekeeper speaking. And by the way, young lady, that is not how one addresses a person on the telephone. First one identifies oneself and then asks for the person with whom one wishes to speak.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: What are you saying?!

lines.speaker: Housekeeper
lines.text: Now, let us try that again, shall we?
The housekeeper hangs up.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: (Shocked) No! Ooh! Oh my god! (Dials again.)

lines.speaker: Housekeeper
lines.text: The Waltham Residence.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: (In a British accent) Hello. This is Phoebe Buffay. I was wondering, please, if-if it's not too much trouble, please, umm, might I speak to Miss Emily Waltham, please?

lines.speaker: Housekeeper
lines.text: Miss Waltham, is at the rehearsal dinner and it's not polite to make fun of people. Goodbye.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: No no no, I'll be nice, I swear!!! Could you just give me the number for where they are?

lines.speaker: Housekeeper
lines.text: I'm afraid, I'm not at liberty to divulge that information.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Ok, somebody is on their way to ruin wedding okay. And I have to warn somebody, alright. So if you don't give me that number then I'm going to come over there and kick your snooty ass all the way to New Glocken..shire.

lines.speaker: Housekeeper
lines.text: Hangs up.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Hello, Hello. Ohh, OHH, she knew I could kick her ass.
Rehearsal dinner hall. Ross and Emily are standing in the reception area. Monica arrives with her parents.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Hey.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: (Ross hugs his mom and dad)Hi. Mom. Dad.

lines.speaker: Mrs. Geller
lines.text: Sweetheart. Oh sorry were late, my fault, I insisted on riding the tube.

lines.speaker: Mr. Geller
lines.text: (embarrassed) Judy, the kids..

lines.speaker: Mrs. Geller
lines.text: Jack, that's what they call the subway.

lines.speaker: Mr. Geller
lines.text: Ohh, I thought that you....

lines.speaker: Ross and Monica
lines.text: Dad, dad. We got it!! We Got It!!!

lines.speaker: Emily
lines.text: Ohh, here comes my dad and stepmum. Mister and Misses Geller, this is Steven and Andrea Waltham.

lines.speaker: Mr. Waltham
lines.text: (Shaking everyone's hand.) Hello. Hello. How do you do? How do you do? Very nice to meet you. (Looking over at his wife.) Darling it's the Gellers. (She pays no attention she's talking on a cellular phone.) (Louder) Darling, it's the Gellers. (She's still not responding.) She's very self-absorbed, you know. I should never have married her.

lines.speaker: Mrs. Waltham
lines.text: (Looking evilly at her husband) Sorry, what?

lines.speaker: Mr. Waltham
lines.text: It's the Gellers!

lines.speaker: Mrs. Waltham
lines.text: Where?

lines.speaker: Mr. Waltham
lines.text: Well there's one (pointing towards Jack) and there's another (pointing towards Judy).

lines.speaker: Mrs. Waltham
lines.text: Lovely to meet you.

lines.speaker: Mr. Waltham
lines.text: Terribly nice of you to offer to pay for half the wedding. (He hands a multipage bill to Jack.)

lines.speaker: Mr. Geller
lines.text: Ohh forget it. Too hell with tradition, we're happy to do it.

lines.speaker: Mrs. Geller
lines.text: We know how expensive weddings can be, besides this may be the only wedding we get to throw (patting Monica on the shoulder.).

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Ha ha, a joke that's funny in all countries.
Ross quickly directs the families to their tables.
Chandler and Joey are standing by the kitchen entrance. A waiter comes out.

lines.speaker: Waiter
lines.text: Sir? (Looking at Joey and holding a tray of food.)

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: What's in it?

lines.speaker: Waiter
lines.text: Goat cheese, water chestnuts, and panchetta. (Joey Looks down disgustingly at the food.)

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: (Looking up at the waiter)That's not food...No, I don't, no...(Taps Chandler on the shoulder.) Everything's different here...I want to go home. I...I miss my family. I miss the coffee house. I can't even remember what Phoebe looks like.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Joey, it's been three days, okay.. Your just a little homesick, Okay. Would you just try to relax. Just, just try to enjoy yourself.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: (Pointing at Chandler.) Your different here too. Your mean in England. (Chandler throws his hands up to his head in frustration. They walk away from each other.)
[Camera pans to the Geller family table. Ross, Rachel, Mr. and Mrs. Geller are there.]

lines.speaker: Mr. Geller
lines.text: (Looking at ht wedding bill.) What the hell!!!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: what's up, Dad?

lines.speaker: Mr. Geller
lines.text: This bill for my half of the wedding. it's insane.

lines.speaker: Mrs. Geller
lines.text: How could it be so much? The receptions at their house.

lines.speaker: Mr. Geller
lines.text: (Pointing items out on the bill.) Flowers, liquor, recarpet first floor. New guest bath, landscaping. I'm paying to remodel this guys house. (Angrily gets up.) I'm going to give that son on a bitch, a piece of my mind.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: (Holding him back.)Dad, dad, please. Look I don't want anything to upset Emily tonight. Alright, she's had a hard enough couple of days as it is. (Picks up the bill.) Now here, here, let me go talk to him, okay?

lines.speaker: Mr. Geller
lines.text: And you tell him no one takes advantage of the Gellers.

lines.speaker: Mrs. Geller
lines.text: Ooh, Jack....(He looks over to her) Sometimes I forget how powerful you can be. (They embrace and kiss passionately.)

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: (Looking nauseous from her parents kissing.) And I'm going to go get drunk. (Gets up to get a drink.)
John F. Kennedy International Airport, Rachel is running to the ticket counter.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Ooh, ooh, ooh,ooh,ooh. (Slightly out of breath) Hi.

lines.speaker: Ticket Agent
lines.text: (Cheerfully.) Hello.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: (Faking cheerfulness.) Hello. Umm, when is your next flight to London?

lines.speaker: Ticket Agent
lines.text: (looking at her computer terminal) There's one leaving in thirty minutes.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Ohh, good.

lines.speaker: Ticket Agent
lines.text: And I do have one seat left.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Ohh, thank you, thank you, thank you.

lines.speaker: Ticket Agent
lines.text: The last minute fare on this ticket is twenty seven hundred dollars.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: (Pause) Huh.. How about 600?

lines.speaker: Ticket Agent
lines.text: Sorry.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: How about 600 and these earrings? (Shows the ticket agent her earrings.)

lines.speaker: Ticket Agent
lines.text: They prefer it if I don't barter.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: (looking through her wallet.) Ohh, I just don't think I have enough left on my credit card.

lines.speaker: Ticket Agent
lines.text: Well you can split it with another credit card.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Ohh, okay, how about five. (She hands her all the credit cards.) Ohh, thank you.

lines.speaker: Ticket Agent
lines.text: I'm just going to need to see your passport.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: (Looking through her purse.) Okay, you know what? I don't have it, but I can tell you exactly where it is on my night stand, and...okay. But you know what? I have my drivers license and I have a twenty. (She slides it across the counter.)

lines.speaker: Ticket Agent
lines.text: (Slides the twenty back and tosses her credit card onto the counter.)
The Rehearsal dinner hall. Ross is at the Walthams' table discussing the bill.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Look, face it, my father is not going to pay for the build-in barbecue and believe me you can kiss you gazebo goodbye. Now I might be able to get you the new lawn.

lines.speaker: Mr. Waltham
lines.text: Ahh, then you have to give us the lawn ornaments.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: I go back there with lawn ornaments, he's going to laugh in my face.

lines.speaker: Mrs. Waltham
lines.text: This is ridiculous. I mean we had an agreement. (Ross looks frustrated. She begins to scream at her husband.) Will you say something, Steven?! Please!!!

lines.speaker: Mr. Waltham
lines.text: Don't take that tone with me. (She looks evilly at him.) All-all right you can. (He looks over at Ross and Shrugs.)
Monica and Rachel's, Phoebe is dialling the phone and Rachel runs in the door.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: (Running to her room.) Hi, Pheebs.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: (Looking relieved. She puts down the phone.) Oh thank god. Oh, you changed your mind. Oh, look I know you probably want to be alone, and you don't want to talk about it, that's fine. I just want you to know, I think you are doing the right thing and...

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: (Running back out the door with her passport.) Bye, Pheebs.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: (Trying to get up.) Wait!! Where are you going?! What are you doing?! No!! Wait!! (Not able to get up.) God!! Why am I always pregnant when she does that?!

The Rehearsal Dinner Hall. Chandler, Joey, Ross, Emily, Monica, and all the bridal party are seated at the table. Chandler gets up to make a toast. He clinks his glass, but the napkin is still in the glass. Needless to say, it sounds weird. He takes out the napkin and clinks his glass again.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: I'd like to toast, Ross and Emily. Of course, my big toast will be tomorrow at the wedding, so this is kind of my little toast or Melba toast, if you will. (No one in the room laughs. He starts to get flustered.) Okay. I known Ross for a long time. In fact, I knew him when he was going out with his first girlfriend. (Ross looks embarrassed.) And I thought things were going to work out for him.. Until the day he over inflated her. (He laughs. Jack looks at Judy and no one in the room laughs.) Ohh, Dear God.. (A cellular phone rings.)

lines.speaker: Mrs. Waltham
lines.text: Hello, Waltham Interiors.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Oh, hi, Mrs. Waltham. I need to speak with ether one of the best men, or Ross's sister Monica.

lines.speaker: Mrs. Waltham
lines.text: Who is this?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Oh, I'm Phoebe Buffay. I'm one of Ross's best friends.

lines.speaker: Mrs. Waltham
lines.text: Where did you get this number?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: I got it from your maid. She's a bitch, but I wore her down.

lines.speaker: Mrs. Waltham
lines.text: Well, if you're one of Ross's best friends, why aren't you here?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Yeah, um, I can't fly. I'm having my brother's babies.

lines.speaker: Mrs. Waltham
lines.text: Oh, am I on the radio?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: No..umm, could I talk to one of them? It's very very important.

lines.speaker: Mrs. Waltham
lines.text: No, I'm bored with you now. I'm going to cut you off. (She hangs up.)

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Ohh! Okay, I'm going to have to kick her ass too.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: (Continuing his toast.) And I'm sure we're all very excited that Ross and Emily are getting married at Montgomery Hall. I mean to think, my friend getting married in Monty Hall. (No reaction from the people.) Ohh, come on!! Monty Hall!! Lets make a Deal!! Come on, you people!! All right, forget it!! Congratulations, Ross and Emily. (He sits down.)

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Hey, best man number two, Joey Tribbiani. Now I'm not good with the jokes like Chandler here. Boy...but ahh, I just want to say congratulation to the happy couple. I first met Ross in this coffee house back home...Home...New York City...Where everybody knows my name. Well anyway, I love you guys. (pointing at everyone.) But not as much as I love America. (Looking at Chandler.) Could we please..go home now?
One of the bridesmaids, Felicity, puts her arm around Joey.

lines.speaker: Felicity
lines.text: (Putting her arm around Joey.) Are you going home? I was hoping to get to know you better.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: (Putting his arm around her.) I'm not going anywhere, sweetheart.
Later that evening at the rehearsal dinner. Chandler and Monica are sitting on a sofa. Chandler is covering his face in embarrassment because of the toast.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: I was laughing. (Patting him on the knee.)

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Out loud?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Well I didn't want everyone to think I was stupid.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: So how are you doing?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: My mother's driving me crazy, but Ross is getting married. I'm happy. (A drunken man approaches.) I'm not going to let anything spoil that.

lines.speaker: Drunk Man
lines.text: I just want to say that Ross is a wonderful young man.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Well, thanks, we like him.

lines.speaker: Drunk Man
lines.text: My god!! You must have been a teenage when you had him. (Monica stares straight forward after the comment. Chandler tries to console her by patting her on the shoulder.)
Ross and Emily's parents are seated at a table. Ross is between them and they are discussing the wedding bill.

lines.speaker: Mr. Geller
lines.text: There's no way in hell, I'm paying for it.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Look, were down to just one point. Could we please, maybe just settle it after the wedding.

lines.speaker: Mr. Geller
lines.text: All-right fine, but I just want to say, I'm not paying for your wine cellar. You thieving, would be speaking German if it weren't for us, cheap little man. (Emily's stepmum looks shocked. Jack and Judy get up and leave.)
Chandler's trying to console Monica.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: The guy was hammered, okay? There's no way, you look like Ross's mother.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Then why would he say it?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Because he's crazy. Okay? He came up to me earlier and thanked me for my very moving performance in Titanic.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Oh, my mother's right. I'm never going to get married.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Ahh, you know what? That is....Who wouldn't want you?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Ohh, Please?! I'm a single mom, with a thirty year old son!!
The airport. Rachel runs up to the ticket counter.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Hi, I'm back. Listen, I need to...

lines.speaker: Ticket Agent
lines.text: Hello.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Hello. I need to get on the 11 o'clock flight.

lines.speaker: Ticket Agent
lines.text: Oh I'm afraid that plane has already pulled away from the gate.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Okay, you know what?  You're going to have to call that plane and tell them to swing around and come and pick me up.

lines.speaker: Ticket Agent
lines.text: I can't do that.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Sure, you know what? Come on, we'll just tell them that there was like a problem with like the "engine".

lines.speaker: Ticket Agent
lines.text: I'm afraid I'm going to have to ask you to step aside, Miss.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Look, If I don't get to London!! He is going to marry that other girl!!!

lines.speaker: Ticket Agent
lines.text: I can't imagine why.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: All right, you know what? I am not leaving here, until you call that plane back!! (She pounds her hand on the counter twice. The ticket agent counters by placing the closed sign on the counter and tapping it twice.)
Chandlers hotel room. Ross bursts into the room.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: (Screaming) I'm getting married today!! Whoo-hoo!!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: (With the covers pulled up to his chin.) Morning, Ross.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: I'm getting married, to..day!!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Yeah you are!!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Ahh, whoo-hoo!!(He runs back out the door.)

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: (Comes up for below the covers and looks concerned.) Do you think he knew I was here? (Chandler quickly looks at Monica not knowing what to say.)
Commercial Break
The Virgin Atlantic flight to London that Rachel is on.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Ohhh.(she rhythmically taps her hands on the magazine on her lap.)

lines.speaker: Passenger
lines.text: Ahh, ahh, excuse me.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Yeah?

lines.speaker: Passenger
lines.text: If you're planning on doing that throughout the entire flight. Please tell me now. So that I could that a sedative...or perhaps slip you one.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh. I'm sorry. I'm very sorry. Sorry. (She hums and sighs happily.) It's just, I'm ahh, I'm kinda excited. I'm, ahh, going to London to ahh, tell this guy that I love him and... (He puts his headphones on to ignore her.)
Chandlers hotel room. Chandler and Monica are lying in the bed together talking. There's an awkward air between them. They are both clutching the covers in from of them.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Well I've-I've never done that with you before.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: (In am uneasy voice.) Nope. (She chuckles uneasily.)

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: So, ahh, how are ya? How ya...How ya... You okay?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Yep, yep...You?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Yes...Yes..Uh-huh, You?(Looking over at her. She looks back.) We did you.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Well...I'd better get going.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Oh yea yea, absolutely.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: (Scoots towards the side of the bed.) Could you not look?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: I don't want to look.
Joey enters his hotel room. The phone is ringing.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Hello?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: (Angrily.) Hey, were the hell have you been?!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Hey. I spent the night out. I met this cute bridesmaid. She is so...

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: I don't want to hear about her!!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Ahh Pheebs, you know you're still my number one girl.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: No! No, we have an emergency. Okay? Rachel's coming to London.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Ohh great!!!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: No it's not great. No, she's coming to tell Ross that she loves him.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: (Confused.) But, he loves Emily?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: I KNOW THAT!!! You have to stop her!! She's going to ruin the wedding!!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Okay.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: All right, so, okay...

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Hold on. Hold on. (Picking up a note pad and writing and reading the message aloud.) Rachel coming. Do...Something.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Okay, so I'm done my part, okay. It's your responsibility now, okay. The burden is off me, right?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Right!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: So tell me about this girl?
The plane. Rachel's telling her story to the passenger on her left. The one on her left is still wearing his headphones.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: ...And so then I realised. All this stuff I had been doing. proposing to Joshua, lying to Ross about why I couldn't come to the wedding. Was all just a way of...

lines.speaker: Passenger
lines.text: (Frustrated he takes his headphones off.) Oh, oh oh!! I'm sorry, can I interrupt? You know I just want to say..That you are a horrible, horrible person.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Ehh, pardon me?

lines.speaker: Passenger
lines.text: You say you love this man, yet you're about to ruin the happiest day of his life. I'm afraid I have to agree with you friend Pheebs.. This is a..this is a...terrible, terrible plan.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: But he has to know how I feel!

lines.speaker: Passenger
lines.text: But why? He loves this...this Emily person. No good can come of this.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: (Sighing) Well I-I think your wrong.

lines.speaker: Passenger
lines.text: Oh-no.(He bites his fist at her.)

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Well, he doesn't really love her. I mean, it's just a rebound thing from me.... You'll see!

lines.speaker: Passenger
lines.text: Fortunately, I won't. And by the way, it seems to be perfectly clear that you were on a break. (Rachel gasps and doesn't know what to say. He puts his headphones back on.)
The guys hotel room. Joey's sitting on his bed, holding the note he wrote while talking to Phoebe. He's patting the note with a pen and staring off into space.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Do something..... Something....
Chandler comes out of the bathroom in a robe.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Hey.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Hey.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Have you seen Monica?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: (Very defensive.) I'm not seeing Monica.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: (With a confused look on his face.) What?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: What?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Look we've got to find her. Phoebe just called!! Rachel's coming to tell Ross she loves him!!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Oh my god!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: I know! That's why we got to find Monica!! You know where she is?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: No!! Okay!! What's with the third degree?! Why don't you just shine a light in my eyes?! (Joey looks totally confused.)
The church where Ross and Emily are to be married. Judy, Andrea, and Monica enter together.

lines.speaker: Mrs. Geller
lines.text: (Looking around at the chapel.) Oh my God! It's like a fairyland.

lines.speaker: Mrs. Waltham
lines.text: I know, it's horrible isn't it?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Well, I love it. I only hope my wedding looks this good.

lines.speaker: Mrs. Geller
lines.text: I just hope...

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: (Angrily.) You can let some of them go by!(Judy and Andrea go to the front of the chapel. Joey approaches Monica.)

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: (Whispering.) Pisst, Monica. Alright, we really need to start looking out for Rachel. I'll cover the front door. You watch that big hole at the back of the building and I got Chandler covering Ross.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: (Awkwardly.) Why would I care where Chandler is? You know uhh...You know sometimes I don't even like Chandler.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Okay. (They both walk off to watch for Rachel.)
Ross and Chandler are standing next to the alter. Ross is practising for the wedding.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: (Using a slightly different inflection for each.) I do. I do. I do.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Oh yea, your right. It's the second one.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: (Very Nervous)Really?
[Scene: Joey's in the front entrance watching for Rachel. The bridesmaid he met at the rehearsal dinner come in.

lines.speaker: Felicity
lines.text: (In a sexy voice.)Hello Joey.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Hey, Felicity.

lines.speaker: Felicity
lines.text: Umm, I thought about you all day.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Yeah.

lines.speaker: Felicity
lines.text: Um-hum. Talk New York to me again.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: (In a New York accent.) Fuggetaboutit. (She giggles.) How you doin?

lines.speaker: Felicity
lines.text: Mmm. (She pushes him up against the wall and they begin to kiss.)

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Oh, yeah.
Back in the chapel. The parents are still fighting over the bill. Ross is refereeing.

lines.speaker: Mrs. Geller
lines.text: There's nothing to discuss. We're not paying for your wine cellar.

lines.speaker: Mr. Waltham
lines.text: (Pleading.) You-you have to meet me in the middle here.

lines.speaker: Mr. Geller
lines.text: (Forcefully.) Hey, you keep pushing me on this, my foots going to meet the middle of your ass.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Dad!! (Emily comes running in.)

lines.speaker: Emily
lines.text: What-what's going on?!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Nothing, nothing. Everything's under control.

lines.speaker: Mr. Waltham
lines.text: You want a piece of me, sir? Is that what your saying? (Pointing at Jack and poking him) You want a piece of me?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: (Stepping in between them.)Okay! Okay! That's it!! Parents!! Parents!! Back away!! All right, this is our wedding day! From now on everyone gets along, and if I hear one more word. NO GRANDCHILDREN! (Pointing at his mother.) That's right!!

lines.speaker: Mr. Geller
lines.text: Okay, okay.

lines.speaker: Mr. Waltham
lines.text: Sorry old boy, sorry. Sorry. Sorry. (Them all walk away. As he leaves he mutters to Jack.) I could kill you with my thumb, you know.

lines.speaker: Emily
lines.text: What was all that about?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: (Sighing.) It was...This disagreement over...(She sighs. Ross notices her in her wedding dress.) My god. You...you look beautiful.

lines.speaker: Emily
lines.text: (Giggles.) Ohh...(She realises that she's in her gown.) Oh! You were not meant to see me before the wedding. It's bad luck.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: You know what, I think we've had all the bad luck we're going to have. (He hugs her.)

The front entrance. Joey and the bridesmaid are up against the wall kissing. Rachel comes in the door and walks by Joey unnoticed. She walks into the chapel and sees Ross and Emily kissing. She looks as though she wants to cry. Emily walks away and Ross turns and sees Rachel standing there.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: My God. Rachel! (He walks towards her, grasps her hands and kisses her on the cheek.) Your here. I can't believe it. (She giggles.) What happen? Why are you here?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Well I just came...(She touches him near his heart. She's almost in tears.) I just needed to tell you...(Looking into his eyes. She takes a deep breath.) Congratulations. (He hugs her. She can barely hold back the tears.)

Camera fades to one of the band members playing guitar at the wedding. The chapel is full of guest. A groomsmen escorts a bridesmaid down the isle. Joey is waiting with Mrs. Waltham to escort he down the isle. A cellular phone rings.

lines.speaker: Mrs. Waltham
lines.text: (Answering the phone.) Hello, Waltham Interiors.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Mrs. Waltham. Hi. It's Phoebe again.

lines.speaker: Mrs. Waltham
lines.text: (Throws her head back in disgust.) Why?!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Yea. Can I please, please, please talk to one of the best men? This is going to be the last time I promise.

lines.speaker: Mrs. Waltham
lines.text: (Slapping the phone into Joey's chest.) Joey there's a girl on the phone for you.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: (Smiling.) Ohh great!! (Putting the phone to his ear.) Hello. (He begins to escort her down the aisle.)

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Joey, it's Phoebe. Did you stop Rachel?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: No, but it's okay. She just came in and gave him a hug, that it.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: So nothing got ruined?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: No.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Oh that's so great! Ohh, so what's going on now?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Ah, I'm-I'm walking down the aisle...Still walking. (Mrs. Waltham takes her place.) I'm about to pass the bridesmaid I hooked up with last night. (Looking at the bridesmaid.) Hey! (Talking to Phoebe.) I told her "Hey." And now I'm at the front with Ross. It's Phoebe. (He shows Ross the phone.) He looks pretty mad. Uh...I'd better go.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: No!! wait, wait, wait!! Oh please, hold it up so I can listen. (Joey looks at Ross and holds the phone above Ross's shoulder.)
Chandler escorts Monica down the aisle.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: What we did last night was....

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Stupid.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Totally crazy stupid. (He nods his head at the people seated.)

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: What were we thinking?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: I'm coming over tonight though, right?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Oh yeah. Definitely.

They quickly take their places and Here Comes the Bride begins to play. Everyone seated looks back. Emily is being escorted up the aisle by her father. She kisses him on the cheek and takes her place by Ross's side.

lines.speaker: Minister
lines.text: Friends. Family. We are gathered to celebrate here today the joyous union of Ross and Emily. May the happiness we share with them today be with them always. Now Emily, repeat after me. I, Emily...

lines.speaker: Emily
lines.text: I, Emily...

lines.speaker: Minister
lines.text: Take thee Ross...

lines.speaker: Emily
lines.text: Take thee Ross...

lines.speaker: Minister
lines.text: As my lawfully wedded husband, in sickness and in health, till death parts us.

lines.speaker: Emily
lines.text: As my lawfully wedded husband, in sickness and in health, until death parts us.

lines.speaker: Minister
lines.text: Now Ross, repeat after me. I Ross...

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: I Ross...

lines.speaker: Minister
lines.text: Take thee, Emily...

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Take thee, Rachel...(All his friends have looks of shock on their faces. He realises what he said. Quickly he says.) Emily. (A slight chuckle.) Emily.
Rachel looks all around as if all the eyes in the chapel were looking at her.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: (To the woman sitting in front of her) He-he said Rachel, right? Do you think I should go up there?

lines.speaker: Minister
lines.text: (Looking and feeling awkward. he looks towards Emily.) Uhh...Shall I go on?
{Transcriber's Note: This is orginally where the season four cliffhanger was.  So you all have to wait four months before reading on.}

lines.speaker: Emily
lines.text: Yes, yes, do go on.
Cut to the parents.

lines.speaker: Mr. Geller
lines.text: (to Mr. Waltham) He better go on for what I'm paying.

lines.speaker: Mr. Waltham
lines.text: After what he just said, you're paying for the whole bloody lot.

lines.speaker: Minister
lines.text: I think we'd better start again. Ross, repeat after me. I, Ross...

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: I, Ross...

lines.speaker: Minister
lines.text: Take thee, EM-I-LY...

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Take thee, (Glares at the Minister) Emily. (Chuckles) Like there'd be anybody else. (Emily is glaring at him.)

lines.speaker: Minister
lines.text: As my lawfully wedded wife, in sickness and in health, till death parts us.
Cut to the Walthams.

lines.speaker: Mrs. Waltham
lines.text: (to Mr. Waltham) Looks like that might come any minute.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: As my lawfully wedded wife, in sickness and in health, until death parts us. Really, I do. Emily. (Points at her.)

lines.speaker: Minister
lines.text: May I have the rings? (He is given the rings) Emily, place this ring on Ross's finger as a symbol of your bond everlasting. (She jams the ring onto his finger) Ross, place this ring in Emily's hand as a symbol of the love that encircles you forever.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Happy to.

lines.speaker: Minister
lines.text: Ross and Emily have made their declarations and it gives me great pleasure to declare them husband and wife.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Yay!

lines.speaker: Minister
lines.text: You may kiss the bride.
He goes to kiss her, but she isn't very receptive of the kiss. She keeps avoiding him, until Ross finally gets to kiss her on her cheek.

lines.speaker: Mrs. Geller
lines.text: (To Mr. Geller) This is worse than when he married the lesbian.
The band starts to play, and the recessional starts. Ross tries to take Emily's hand, but she snatches it away from him.

lines.speaker: Emily
lines.text: Just keep smiling.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Okay.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Well, that went well. Yeah.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: It could've been worse, he could've shot her.
Ross and Emily make it to the lobby.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: (laughs) That uh, that was pretty funny. Wasn't it?
Emily gives him a forearm shot across the stomach.
Commercial Break
The Wedding reception, Ross and Emily are in the bathroom and Emily is yelling at him. Rachel, Chandler, Joey, and Monica are standing outside the doorway.

lines.speaker: Emily
lines.text: (Yelling from inside the bathroom) You've spoiled everything! It's like a nightmare! My friends and family are out there! How can I face them?! How can you do this to me?!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: (To the gang) Hey, no matter what happens with Ross and Emily, we still get cake right?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: (exiting the bathroom) That-that-that's all right, no honey, you take your time sweetie. I'll be right out here. (She slams the door in his face, to the gang) She's just fixing her makeup.

lines.speaker: Emily
lines.text: I hate you!!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: And, I love you!! (He walks into the living room)

lines.speaker: Mr. Geller
lines.text: Boy, bad time to say the wrong name, huh Ross?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: That's true, thanks dad. (To All) People should be dancing! Huh? Hey, this is a party! Come on! Joey, dance!! (He starts to dance but stops when no one else joins him.)
Mrs. Waltham's phone rings and she answers it.

lines.speaker: Mrs. Waltham
lines.text: Yes, Waltham interiors.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: (On the phone, in New York) Uh, hello, this is Ross Geller's personal physician, Dr. Philange.

lines.speaker: Mrs. Waltham
lines.text: Who?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Yeah, I've discovered that Ross forgot to take his brain medicine, uh, now without it, uh, in the brain of Ross, uh women's names are interchangeable, through-through no fault of his own.

lines.speaker: Mrs. Waltham
lines.text: Oh my God, Phoebe.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: No, not Phoebe, Dr. Philange. Oh no! You have it too!
Mrs. Waltham hangs up on her.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Hello? What kind of bitch hangs up on a doctor!
Cut to Chandler and Monica at the buffet table.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Hey.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Hey.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Oh wow, I hope you don't take this the wrong way but, I know we had plans to meet up tonight and, ugh, I'm just kinda worried about what it might do to our friendship.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: I know. How could we have let this happen?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Seven times!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Ugh! Well, y'know, we were away...

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: In a foreign, romantic country...

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: I blame London.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Bad London! (Takes a spoon and smacks the turkey.)

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: So look umm, while we're st-still in London, I mean, we can keep doing it right?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Well, I don't see that we have a choice. But, when we're back home, we don't do it.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Only here.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Y'know, I saw a wine cellar downstairs...

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: I'll meet you there in two minutes.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Okay!
He throws down his plate and runs to the wine cellar, Monica is about to follow him but is intercepted by Rachel.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Mon, honey, I gotta ask you something.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: (impatiently) Now?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Ross said my name up there, I mean, come on, I just can't pretend that didn't happen can I?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Oh, I-I don't know.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Monica, what should I do?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Just uh, do the right thing. (Uses some breath spray)

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: What?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Toe the line. Thread the needle. Think outside the box! (Tries to leave, but is stopped by Rachel.)

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Whoa, wait, listen, I think I'm just gonna talk to Ross about what he think it meant.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Wait. Rachel, no, he's married. Married! If you don't realise that, I can't help you.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Okay, you're right. You're right. You can't help me.
Cut to Mr. and Mrs. Geller.

lines.speaker: Mrs. Geller
lines.text: Jack, is it all our fault? Were we bad parents?

lines.speaker: Mr. Waltham
lines.text: (walking by) Yes.

lines.speaker: Mr. Geller
lines.text: Oh yeah, well who serves steak when there's no place to sit, I mean how are you supposed to eat this?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Hey, what's up? (He has solved the problem of eating the steak, he's eating it with his hands.)

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: (motions to himself and Mrs. Geller and grins) You and me, next dance?
Cut to Rachel who is walking past Mrs. Waltham.

lines.speaker: Mrs. Waltham
lines.text: Sweetheart, sweetheart...

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Yes?

lines.speaker: Mrs. Waltham
lines.text: You know, we're all wondering who this Rachel is. Can you point her out to me?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh, you know what? Rachel.. She just left. (Points at the entrance.)

lines.speaker: Mrs. Waltham
lines.text: Oh.. Never mind. Who are you?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Ra.... Er.. Barbra.

lines.speaker: Mrs. Waltham
lines.text: Red Barbra?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Yeah. (Smiles uneasily.)
Cut to Monica and Chandler, Monica is running up to him.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Where were you? We were supposed to meet in the wine cellar?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Forget it, that's off.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Why?!

lines.speaker: Mr. Waltham
lines.text: (drunkenly) The next tour of the wine cellar will plan in two in-in minutes...
Joey walks up to them.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Joey, what are you doing? You promised Phoebe you wouldn't eat meat until she has the babies!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Well, I figured we're in another country, so it doesn't count.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: That's true.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: The man's got a point.
Cut to Rachel and Ross.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh, hi!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Hi!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Hi. Sorry, things aren't working out so well.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Oh no! It could be better, but it's gonna be okay, right?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh yeah! Of course, I mean, she's gonna get over this, y'know? I mean, so you said my name! Y'know you just said it 'cause you saw me there, if you'd have seen a circus freak, you would've said, "I take thee circus freak." Y'know, it didn't mean anything, it's just a mistake. It didn't mean anything. Right?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: No! No! Of course it didn't mean anything! I mean, uh well, I can understand why Emily would think it meant something, y'know, because-because it was you...

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Right...

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: But it absolutely didn't. (Yelling towards the bathroom) It didn't!! It didn't!!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: (approaching) Ross, hey, the band's ready outside for your first dance with Emily, so...

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: (sarcastic) Oh! Oh-oh, the band's ready! Well, I-I-we gotta do what the band says-I don't care about the stupid band!!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: You spit on me man! (Wipes his face.)

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Look, I'm sorry.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Emily is kinda taking a long time, huh?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: (laughs) Y'know when I locked myself in the bathroom at my wedding, it was because I was trying to pop the window out of the frame.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Oh, right!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Get the hell out of there, y'know?
They all start laughing, and quickly stop when they realise what she just said and run over to the bathroom.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: (Bangs on the bathroom door) Emily? Emily? I'm coming in. (He opens the door to reveal that the window is gone, along with Emily.)

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Well, look at that, same thing.
London Marriott, Monica and Chandler are walking to her room.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Listen, in the middle of everything if I scream the word, "Yippee!" just ignore me.
She laughs and opens the door to reveal Rachel sitting on the bed.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Oh my God, Rachel! Hi!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Oh, hello Rachel.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Ross said my name. Okay? My name.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: How did you get in here?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: If anybody asks, I'm your sister, Joan. Listen. C'mon, you guys. Ross said my name up there that obviously means that he still loves me!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: (to Monica) Your sister Joan is crazy.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: (They both just stare at her.)Y'know what? Fine, I don't care. Don't believe me, I know I'm right-do you guys want to go downstairs and get a drink?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Yes, we do. But, we have to change first.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Yes, I want to change. And why-why don't you go down and get us a table?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Yeah, we'll be down in like five minutes.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: (elbows him) Fifteen minutes.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Okay.
The phone rings and Rachel answers it.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Hello? Oh, Pheebs! (To them) It's Phoebe!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Oh, yay...

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Great...

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Hi!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Hi, so what happened?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Well, Ross said my name.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Yeah, I know, but I don't think that means anything.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Okay, Pheebs, y'know what, let's look at this objectively all right? Ninth grade, right? The obsession starts. All right? The summer after ninth grade he sees me in a two-piece for the first time, his obsession begins to grow. So then...

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: (To Monica) Hey, listen, why don't we go change in my room?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: But my clothes are-ohh! (They both leave.)
Cut to Chandler's room, he opens the door slowly to see if Joey is there and after seeing that he isn't, ushers Monica into the room, closes the door, and the security bar.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Wow, you look...

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: No time for that!
They both start to frantically rip each other's clothes off, but are interrupted when Joey tries to open the door.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Hey, dude, let me in. I got a girl out here!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Well, I've got a girl in here.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: No you don't, I just saw you go in there with Monica!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Well, we're-we're hanging out in here!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Look, which one of us is gonna be having sex in there, me or you?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Well, I suppose I'd have to say you!! But, what if we're watching a movie in here?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Which we are, and-and we already paid for it. It's My Giant!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: My Giant? I love that movie!
Ross and Emily's room, Chandler and Monica are still looking for a place to do the deed.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: You really think this is okay?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Well, Ross and Emily aren't gonna use it.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Oh, it's so beautiful. Ohh! Y'know, I-I don't know if I feel right about this.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Oh Mon-Mon-Mon-Mon-look, this is the honeymoon suite. The room expects sex. The room would be disappointed if it didn't get sex. All of the other honeymoon suites would think it was a loser.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Okay!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Okay!
They both run to rip the covers off the bed, but are interrupted by Ross.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: (entering) Emily?!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Nope, not under here!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: You didn't find her?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: No, I've looked everywhere!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Well, you couldn't have looked everywhere or else you would've found her!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Yeah, I think you should keep looking!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Yeah, for about 30 minutes.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Or 45.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Wow, in 45 minutes you can find her twice. (Monica smiles at that.)

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: No! For all I know, she's trying to find me but couldn't because I kept moving around. No, from now on, I'm staying in one place. (He sits down on the bed.) Right here.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Well, it's getting late.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Yeah, we're gonna go.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Actually, do you guys mind staying here for a while?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Ugh, y'know, umm we gotta get up early and catch that plane for New York.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Yeah, it's a very large plane.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: (disappointed) That's cool.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: But, we'll stay here with you.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Thanks guys! (They both sit down on either side of him.) I really appreciate this, y'know, but you don't have to rub my butt.
Chandler slowly takes his hand away.
Commercial Break
Ross and Emily's room, the next morning. Ross is now asleep and has his head in Monica's lap and his feet on Chandler's lap. Monica and Chandler are both still awake and depressed.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: We have to leave for New York in an hour.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: I know, I've been looking at those doors, they look pretty sound proof, don't you think?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: We can't do that that's insane. I mean 'A' he could wake up and 'B' y'know, let's go for it.
They both try to slowly extricate themselves from Ross, but there's a knock on the door that awakens him.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Em-Emily? (Looking around for her.) Em-Emily? (He runs to the door.) Emily! (He opens the door to reveal the Walthams standing outside.)

lines.speaker: Mr. Waltham
lines.text: No.

lines.speaker: Mrs. Waltham
lines.text: You can forget about Emily, she's not with us.

lines.speaker: Mr. Waltham
lines.text: We've come for her things.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Wait, well wh-wh-wh-where is she?

lines.speaker: Mr. Waltham
